violations.description: SANITIZATION OF EQUIP. AND UTENSILS (4 pts): Chlorine sanitizing levels observed greater than 200 ppm in the three compartment sink.

inspections.comments: Reminder of proper cooling methods. Potentially hazardous foods must be cooled from 140 degrees fahrenheit to 70 degrees fahrenheit within 2 hours and from 70 degrees fahrenheit to 41 degrees fahrenheit or below within 4 hours. A longer time for cooling allows an ideal situation for bacterial growth. This has been shown to be a major contributing factor in many foodbourne illnesses.

violations.description: CONTAMINATION FROM WIPING CLOTHS (2 pts): Wiping cloths noted laying on counter in kitchen.
TEMPERATURE MEASURING DEVICES (1 pts): Food thermometers not available at time of inspection.
